death_toll_note: Government figure (4,930) via OCHA SitRep #23 (July 16, 9pm) / BNO News (July 17). Cumulative official toll progression: 1,719 (June 29) -> 1,943 (June 30) -> 2,295 (July 1) -> 2,595 (July 2) -> 2,645 (July 3) -> 2,954 (July 4, SitRep #11) -> 3,342 (July 5/6) -> 3,535 (July 7) -> 3,685 (July 8) -> 3,811 (July 9) -> 3,889 (July 10) -> 4,118 (July 11 early) -> 4,333 (July 11 late/SitRep #18) -> 4,490 (July 12/SitRep #19) -> 4,561 (July 13/SitRep #20) -> 4,734 (July 14/SitRep #21) -> 4,829 (July 15/SitRep #22) -> 4,930 (July 16/SitRep #23). Injured: 16,740 (unchanged). Rescued: 6,462 (unchanged). Patients treated: 35,781 (up from 34,872). Families assisted: 128,324. Aftershocks: 1,308 (up from 1,284 SitRep #22). CORE/Wayuu Taya rapid needs assessment (July 14): housing damage at 20 of 24 sites across 7 states, highest-priority in La Guaira and Distrito Capital. Desaparecidos THV tracking site: ~31,000 unaccounted for as of July 5. Venezuela Te Busca: 18,145 still missing as of July 8 (44,243 registered, 26,098 located). UN OCHA: >50,000 missing per Tom Fletcher. NYT: Two doctors at Caracas main morgue estimated true dead closer to 4,000; UN procuring 10,000 body bags. Wikipedia: 16,300 lost homes, 28,300 uninhabitable. USGS OAF (July 16, model run): 1-week M6+ prob 0.90%, M7+ 0.07%; 1-month M6+ 3.15%, M7+ 0.25%; 1-year M6+ 11.49%, M7+ 1.21%. New UN-backed assessment: $37 billion direct physical damage (Miami Herald/AP July 3), superseding earlier $6.7B ECLAC figure. UN OCHA launched US$299 million additional appeal (SitRep #23, July 16, confirmed via HRP Addendum). Tom Fletcher (UN humanitarian chief) concluded Venezuela visit, called for coordinated recovery plan and announced $299M HRP addendum for 1.3M people over 6 months. Total HRP funding requirements rise from $632M to $931M. Delcy Rodriguez renewed calls for release of gold held by Bank of England.

injured.source: Venezuela Information Ministry official update (Sunday July 5/6) via BNO News

missing_independent.source: Desaparecidos Terremoto Venezuela tracking website - ~31,000 reported missing as of July 5 per Wikipedia (decrease from 38,500 as people located)

displaced.source: OCHA SitRep #14 (July 7/8) / Jorge Rodriguez (National Assembly, July 8) - 17,907 people without housing, up from 17,854

affected_total.source: OCHA SitRep #8 (July 1) — 26,403 people affected, incl. 12,841 displaced and severely affected

aftershocks.source: OCHA SitRep #23 (July 16) — 1,308 aftershocks reported, up from 1,284 (SitRep #22)

buildings_collapsed.source: Jorge Rodríguez (National Assembly, July 8) — 190 completely destroyed; 856 sustained significant damage per TASS

buildings_damaged_total.source: Jorge Rodríguez (National Assembly) July 3 — 885 buildings affected, 189 completely collapsed; also per OCHA SitRep #10 and Anadolu Agency

buildings_affected_un.source: UN ECLAC/PAHO assessment (via Bloomberg) — 1.7 million buildings affected, $6.7 billion direct damage

buildings_damaged_satellite.source: Satellite image analysis (NYT June 29) — 58,870 buildings likely damaged or destroyed; Oregon State University analysis via ABC News estimated nearly 60,000

economic_damage_usd.source: New UN-backed assessment (via Miami Herald/AP/Reuters July 3) — $37 billion direct physical damage; supersedes earlier $6.7B ECLAC figure

infrastructures_affected_total.source: OCHA SitRep #5 — includes 38 hospitals and 774 buildings

aftershock_prob_mw6.source: USGS Aftershock Forecast (1-week window, model run July 15, 16:00 UTC). 1-month M6+ 3.15%. 1-year M6+ 11.49%. Probability continuing to decline.

aftershock_prob_mw7.source: USGS Aftershock Forecast (1-week window, model run July 15, 16:00 UTC). 1-month M7+ 0.25%. 1-year M7+ 1.21%. 1-week chance 0.07%.

zones.name: La Guaira
zones.description: Coastal port city, worst-affected urban area. 100+ buildings collapsed per UN OCHA. Vantor before/after satellite imagery available. Port and airport infrastructure severely damaged.
key_infrastructure: Port of La Guaira (critical cargo hub)
imagery_sources: Vantor Open Data — before/after pairs (June 22 vs June 25)
zones.damage_notes: Entire blocks flattened in port area. Buildings constructed before modern seismic codes. Concrete frame structures with masonry infill — catastrophic failure in many cases.

zones.name: Catia La Mar
zones.description: Adjacent to Maiquetia airport. Microsoft AI for Good Lab published a building damage assessment dataset using satellite imagery. Featured in NYT destruction analysis.
zones.damage_notes: Microsoft AI damage assessment model predictions available. Filter recommendation: damage_pct_0m >= 0.8 due to orthorectification issues.

zones.name: Caracas
zones.description: Capital city. Heavy damage in southeastern districts (Los Palos Grandes, Catia). Nearly all high-rise buildings in SE Caracas sustained heavy damage or collapsed per Miyamoto. Stadium damage during baseball game. Hospitals evacuated.
zones.damage_notes: Miyamoto: 'In parts of southeastern Caracas, nearly all high-rise buildings sustained heavy damage or collapsed entirely.' Los Palos Grandes district particularly affected.

zones.name: Morón
zones.description: Near epicentre (Carabobo State). Rural/semi-urban area close to both mainshock epicentres. Significant damage expected given proximity.
zones.damage_notes: Proximity to epicentre suggests severe shaking. Limited media coverage — likely underreported.

zones.name: San Felipe
zones.description: Yaracuy state capital. Both mainshock epicentres centred near here (updated: epicentres near Yumare, 23km SE). High shaking intensity.
zones.damage_notes: Both M7.2 and M7.5 epicentres nearby. Likely experienced MMI VIII-IX shaking.

zones.name: Maiquetia
zones.description: Airport area. Main international airport closed. Vantor imagery shows building damage.
zones.damage_notes: Airport damage severe enough to close main international gateway. Vantor has April 30 pre-event and June 25 post-event imagery.

zones.name: Valencia
zones.description: Major industrial city 59km from M7.5 epicentre. Moderate shaking but significant population exposure.
zones.damage_notes: 59km from epicentre. Lower damage but large population means significant overall impact.
zones.status_note: Moderate damage. Check structures before entering.

tracks.name: Satellite Damage Assessment
tracks.description: Classify building damage from before/after satellite imagery into structured GeoJSON for responders.
tracks.long_description: Using xView2-compatible models (ResNet/U-Net) on satellite imagery to classify buildings as no-damage / minor / major / destroyed. Targeting Sentinel-2 (10m, free), Planet (3m), and Maxar (0.3m) imagery. Output damage polygons for HOT-OSM and UN OCHA.
milestones: Baseline xView2 inference on Catia La Mar

tracks.name: Humanitarian Mapping (HOT-OSM)
tracks.description: Automate building and road footprint extraction for OpenStreetMap.
tracks.long_description: HOT-OSM formally activated with fAIr AI-assisted mapping tool. Running segmentation models for building footprints and road network extraction. Push validated features to OSM via HOT-OSM Tasking Manager for responder use.
milestones: Check HOT-OSM Tasking Manager for projects
Run building segmentation on Caracas/La Guaira

tracks.name: Road Network & Logistics
tracks.description: Classify road passability and optimise aid delivery routes.
tracks.long_description: Extract road graph from OSM, classify each segment as intact/debris-blocked/bridge-collapsed/unknown from post-event imagery. Build weighted graph for vehicle routing to optimise aid distribution based on damage density and population data.
tracks.output: Weighted road network graph with passability scores
milestones: Pull OSM road network for affected area
Integrate with logistics cluster (WFP / UN OCHA)

tracks.name: Thermal / SAR Detection
tracks.description: Detect survivors in rubble using ML on thermal or acoustic data.
tracks.long_description: Stretch goal: drone-mounted thermal cameras for human heat signature detection, acoustic/seismic signal classification for tapping/breathing under rubble, WiFi/Bluetooth sniffing for phones. Requires SAR team partnership for deployment.

tracks.name: Aftershock Forecasting
tracks.description: ML-enhanced ETAS models for aftershock sequence prediction.
tracks.long_description: Longer-term research contribution. Train on historical seismicity catalogs (northern Venezuela, subduction zones). Features: time since mainshock, distance from epicentre, b-value, stress transfer. Model: point process neural nets or transformer on event sequences. Output: probability maps for 24-72h windows.

tracks.name: Missing Persons Aggregation
tracks.description: Aggregate scattered missing-person reports into a structured, searchable database.
tracks.long_description: 50,000+ reported missing. Scrape social media (X, Instagram, Facebook) for Spanish-language missing persons posts. Aggregate into unified database: name, age, description, last known location, photo URL, contact info. Deduplicate and cross-reference with found-persons lists. Text-based aggregation only — no facial recognition without institutional oversight.
milestones: Phase 3: Research paper on ethical framework

gaps.description: High-res building-level damage maps needed for rescue prioritisation
gaps.what_needed: VHR satellite imagery (0.3-1m) or drone surveys over entire urban area

gaps.description: No systematic damage classification per building — ad-hoc reporting only
gaps.what_needed: ML damage classification model output → GeoJSON damage polygons
Road passability assessment for port access corridors

gaps.description: Microsoft AI4G dataset exists but needs validation and extension
gaps.what_needed: Ground-truth validation of AI4G predictions + extension to broader area

gaps.description: Large urban area with heavy damage — no systematic building-level assessment
gaps.what_needed: Scalable damage classification covering 3M population urban area

gaps.description: OSM data quality unknown for affected barrios
gaps.what_needed: Building footprint extraction and validation for informal settlements

gaps.description: Metro system damage unknown; road blockages from collapsed buildings
gaps.what_needed: Road network passability map + alternative route planning

gaps.description: No known high-res imagery available for this zone
gaps.what_needed: Satellite imagery acquisition for epicentre zone assessment

gaps.description: No known high-res imagery available — epicentre zone
gaps.what_needed: Satellite imagery acquisition for epicentre zone

gaps.description: No operational aftershock forecast for Venezuela-specific fault system
gaps.what_needed: ML-enhanced ETAS model calibrated to Bocono fault system

gaps.description: 50,000+ missing — no unified searchable database across fragmented platforms
gaps.what_needed: Aggregation, deduplication, and cross-referencing of scattered reports

gaps.description: InSAR ground displacement data not yet processed for ML use
gaps.what_needed: InSAR coherence/change detection pipeline for ground displacement mapping
